display.purpose: Empty states, welcome screens. Appears when there's nothing else on screen.

headline.purpose: Page titles (Search Results, Your Lists). One per page.

title.purpose: Section headings, results headers, card group labels.

body.purpose: All prose, table cells, email preview text. max-width: 65ch.

label.purpose: Table column headers, field hints, hotel card metadata. The workhorse.

caption.purpose: Chip counts, status tags, timestamp dates. Use sparingly.

shadows.name: micro
shadows.purpose: Toggle group active pill. Barely-there separation.

shadows.name: low
shadows.purpose: Hotel cards and map container at rest.

shadows.name: lifted
shadows.purpose: Search hero card — the primary workspace entry point.

shadows.name: hover
shadows.purpose: Hotel card on hover, paired with translateY(-4px).

shadows.name: popup
shadows.purpose: Map detail popup. Highest in-page z-stack.

motion.name: fast
motion.purpose: Nav item backgrounds, button hover, tab transitions.

motion.name: standard
motion.purpose: Card hover transform+shadow, chevron rotation, spinner.

motion.name: enter
motion.purpose: FadeUp entrance for hotel cards (opacity + translateY).

motion.name: shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton loading animation — warm gradient sweep.

breakpoints.name: md
breakpoints.purpose: Sidebar switches from drawer to sticky. Top bar hidden.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Forest primary action — the default commit button across the app.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background:#44503A; color:#FBF5EA; font-family:'Quicksand',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:14px; padding:14px 20px; border:none; border-radius:12px; cursor:pointer; transition:background .15s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background:#363F2E; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline:2px solid #44503A; outline-offset:2px; }

components.name: Terracotta Action
components.description: Secondary CTA with energy — Compose Emails, active pill tabs.
components.css: .ds-btn-terracotta { background:#C96E3C; color:#FBF5EA; font-family:'Quicksand',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:13px; padding:9px 18px; border:none; border-radius:12px; cursor:pointer; transition:background .15s; } .ds-btn-terracotta:hover { background:#A85A30; }

components.name: Pill Tab
components.description: Property-type tabs on Search; terracotta when active.
components.html: 🏨 Hotels 🏡 Boutique Stays
components.css: .ds-pill { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; gap:7px; background:#FFFCF4; color:#2B2722; font-size:13px; font-weight:600; padding:9px 18px; border:1.5px solid #DDD0B8; border-radius:24px; } .ds-pill-active { background:#C96E3C; color:#FBF5EA; border-color:transparent; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Floating search bar on the sand hero band.
components.css: .ds-input-wrap { display:flex; align-items:center; gap:10px; background:#FFFCF4; border:1px solid rgba(43,39,34,.08); border-radius:16px; padding:0 18px; box-shadow:0 14px 34px -22px rgba(120,80,30,.4); } .ds-input { border:none; outline:none; background:transparent; font-family:'Nunito Sans',sans-serif; font-size:14.5px; color:#2B2722; padding:15px 0; min-width:220px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color:#B6AD9C; }

components.name: Hotel Card
components.description: Search result card — photo, name, address, rating, add-to-list.
components.html:
🏨
The Olive & Stone
Alfama, Lisbon
components.css: .ds-hotel-card { width:260px; background:#FFFCF4; border:1px solid #DDD0B8; border-radius:16px; overflow:hidden; box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(140,90,30,.10); transition:transform .2s, box-shadow .2s; font-family:'Nunito Sans',sans-serif; } .ds-hotel-card:hover { transform:translateY(-4px); box-shadow:0 12px 32px rgba(140,90,30,.15); } .ds-hotel-img { height:150px; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; font-size:40px; background:linear-gradient(135deg,#F3E7CF,#DDD0B8); } .ds-hotel-body { padding:14px 16px; } .ds-hotel-name { font-size:15px; font-weight:700; color:#2B2722; margin-bottom:3px; } .ds-hotel-addr { font-size:12px; color:#9C9388; margin-bottom:10px; } .ds-hotel-btn { width:100%; padding:9px 0; background:transparent; border:1.5px solid #44503A; color:#44503A; font-family:'Quicksand',sans-serif; font-weight:700; font-size:12.5px; border-radius:10px; cursor:pointer; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar nav on the forest ground; amber tint when active.
components.css: .ds-nav { background:#44503A; padding:12px; border-radius:12px; width:200px; font-family:'Nunito Sans',sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { padding:10px 12px; border-radius:10px; font-size:14px; font-weight:500; color:rgba(251,245,234,.6); cursor:pointer; transition:background .15s; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background:rgba(251,245,234,.1); } .ds-nav-active { background:rgba(224,149,74,.2); color:#F4C97A; }

components.name: Status Chips
components.description: Outreach state — the only sanctioned non-brand colours.
components.html: DM SENT ✓ REPLIED NOT SENT
components.css: .ds-chip { display:inline-flex; align-items:center; font-family:'Nunito Sans',sans-serif; font-size:10px; font-weight:700; letter-spacing:.04em; padding:3px 9px; border-radius:24px; } .ds-chip-dm { background:rgba(193,53,132,.12); color:#C13584; } .ds-chip-replied { background:#DCFCE7; color:#166534; } .ds-chip-none { background:#F3E7CF; color:#9C9388; }

narrative.overview:
Dapples is the hour before sunset, indoors: warm light falling in patches across a workspace. It is the opposite of the cold blue productivity tool — the surface is cream and sand, the actions are deep forest, and the accents are the amber and olive of light through leaves. The interface is calm and unhurried, but never precious: the creator is doing real work — finding properties, writing pitches, chasing replies — and the chrome recedes so their work comes forward. Every design decision should answer: does this feel like late-afternoon light, or like software?

The color strategy is **restrained**: cream and sand grounds carry the surface, deep forest carries every primary action, and amber/olive accents activate only the things that matter (current state, brand identity, positive progress). Nothing on this tool fights for attention. Hierarchy is earned through weight, spacing, and the controlled use of color — not size competitions or decorative flourish.

Notion is the reference for editorial calm; the anti-references are clear. This is not generic SaaS blue (HubSpot's exhausted CRM blue, enterprise density). It is not creator-pink softness (Flodesk, Later). It is not a travel booking engine (consumer UI conventions don't apply here). It is a tool a creator reaches for between trips — purposeful, premium, and invisible.
keyCharacteristics: Deep-forest sidebar anchors authority without aggression
Cream canvas body keeps the workspace inviting, not clinical
Amber and terracotta used only where they matter: active states, brand identity, progress
Quicksand + Nunito Sans pair on a real contrast axis — geometric display, humanist body
Shadows are warm-tinted, not generic black — every drop of depth belongs to the brand

rules.name: Status-Only
rules.body: `status-*` and `brand-instagram` are reserved for state and third-party identity — a status green never becomes a decorative accent, and Instagram magenta never appears on anything that isn't Instagram. Everything else on a Dapples surface comes from the golden-hour palette.

rules.name: Warm Shadow
rules.body: Every `box-shadow` uses a warm base — `rgba(140,90,30, ...)` for ambient depth, `rgba(120,80,30, ...)` for lifted surfaces — never generic `rgba(0,0,0, ...)`. A cold shadow on a golden-hour surface reads as a different product.

rules.name: Two Family
rules.body: **Quicksand** (rounded geometric) carries display — the wordmark, headings, numerals in stat tiles, and button labels. **Nunito Sans** (humanist) carries everything else. Two families, paired on a real contrast axis (geometric vs humanist); do not introduce a third. Hierarchy within each comes from weight and size, not from more families.

rules.name: 500-On-Forest
rules.body: Any text on the forest sidebar must be weight 500 or above. The perceived weight of light text on a dark ground drops by one notch; compensate.

rules.name: Flat-By-Default
rules.body: Surfaces are shadowless at rest unless they are interactive cards or floating elements. Do not add shadows to headers, nav items, input fields, or static sections for "depth". Depth is earned by position in the hierarchy, not sprinkled for decoration.

dos: Do use Dapple Forest (`#44503A`) for primary actions, and reserve Terracotta/Amber for active and selected states. Their scarcity is what makes them work.
Do use warm-tinted shadows (`rgba(140,90,30,...)` / `rgba(120,80,30,...)`) for all `box-shadow` declarations. Black shadows are prohibited.
Do maintain weight 500 or above for any text on the forest sidebar.
Do use Quicksand for display and Nunito Sans for body — and nothing else. Weight contrast creates hierarchy within each; a third family does not.
Do use the Dapple Border (`#DDD0B8`) for all input outlines and card strokes. Cool-gray borders are off-brand.
Do apply `border-radius: 16px` to cards and map containers. Roundness communicates approachability without softness.
Do keep the terracotta accent to ≤2 simultaneous on-screen uses. If you see it on a button, a nav pill, AND a badge at once, remove one.
donts: Don't use generic SaaS blue (Material blue, Bootstrap primary, any blue at all outside the `status-sent` chip). This tool must not look like HubSpot or Salesforce.

Don't use pastel, soft, or pink-adjacent tones as UI color. No lavender, no influencer-palette softness. The only magenta permitted is `brand-instagram`, and only on Instagram surfaces. Amber is warm, not sweet.
Don't use consumer travel UI patterns (large hero images, star-rating rows, booking-engine forms). The audience is a creator doing B2B outreach, not a tourist booking a room.
Don't apply `border-left` greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe. Background tint or no treatment — never a left-side colored bar.
Don't use gradient text (`background-clip: text`). Emphasis is weight and size.
Don't add shadows to static content (headers, nav items, page sections). Shadows signal interactivity or elevation — they are not decoration.
Don't introduce a third typeface. No serif for "warmth", no mono for "tech credibility" — Quicksand and Nunito Sans are the whole system.
Don't put Terracotta or Amber on inactive or default states. They are reserved for the active, selected, or primary condition only.
Don't use cool-gray neutrals (no `#e2e8f0`, no generic gray `#999` family). All neutrals must come from the golden-hour ramp.
